heroDesc: Precision machining service for prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components
bodyTitle: CNC Machining Service from drawing review to first article
bodyDesc: CNC machining service focuses on dimensions, assembly and material performance for functional parts. Engineers review datums, tolerances, material, heat treatment and surface requirements before selecting milling, turning or mill-turn routes. Final accuracy, lead time and cost depend on part geometry and drawing review.

panels.title: Service Focus
panels.subtitle: Dimension and Fit
panels.body: Machining and inspection plans are built around holes, datums, threads and mating faces.

panels.title: Process Choice
panels.subtitle: Milling / Turning
panels.body: Route selection depends on shape, hole patterns, rotational features and fixturing risk.

panels.title: Documents
panels.subtitle: Order-Based
panels.body: FAI, dimensional, material or finishing records can be provided as required.

tableRows: Check material, tolerance, datum and finish requirements
Critical dimensions should be confirmed when 2D tolerances are missing
Complex parts may require multiple setups
Caliper, height gauge, CMM or custom gauge
Inspection scope follows drawing and order agreement
Dimensional change after finishing should be reviewed

steps.title: Submit drawings
steps.body: Provide 3D model, 2D tolerances, material, quantity and finish requirements.

steps.title: Engineering review
steps.body: Check fixturing, tool access, tolerance risk and inspection method.

steps.title: Machining and first article
steps.body: Start stable machining and in-process checks after first-article confirmation.

steps.title: Clean and deliver
steps.body: Complete deburring, cleaning, packaging and agreed quality documents.

asideCta.title: Submit Requirements
asideCta.body: Upload drawings and project goals so engineers can review the service route.

related.title: Related Service Combinations
related.desc: Complex projects often combine multiple services. Review by geometry, volume, material and delivery goals.

items.title: Molding Service
items.body: Injection molding, tool trials, low-volume molding and design optimization for plastic parts.

items.title: Custom Manufacturing Service
items.body: Coordinates CNC, sheet metal, finishing, purchased parts and assembly for complex projects.

items.title: Metal Stamping Service
items.body: Single-stage or progressive stamping for brackets, springs, housings and connectors.

faq.title: CNC Machining Service FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is CNC Machining Service suitable for?
items.a: CNC Machining Service is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a CNC Machining Service quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for CNC Machining Service?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
